steps.title: Identify the daily growth rate
steps.content:
From the table, calculate the ratio of bacteria from each day to the next.

Day 1 to Day 2:
$$\frac{5.0 \times 10^5}{2.5 \times 10^5} = 2$$

Day 2 to Day 3:
$$\frac{1.0 \times 10^6}{5.0 \times 10^5} = 2$$

The bacteria doubles each day.

steps.title: Set up the exponential equation and isolate the exponent
steps.content:
With initial amount $2.5 \times 10^5$ on Day 1 and doubling factor 2 per day, the formula is:
$$2.5 \times 10^5 \cdot 2^{d-1} = 5.12 \times 10^8$$

Divide both sides by $2.5 \times 10^5$:
$$2^{d-1} = \frac{5.12 \times 10^8}{2.5 \times 10^5} = \frac{5.12}{2.5} \times 10^3 = 2.048 \times 10^3 = 2048$$

steps.title: Recognize the power of 2 and solve
steps.content:
Since $2048 = 2^{11}$:
$$2^{d-1} = 2^{11}$$

Equating exponents: $d - 1 = 11$, so $d = 12$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
